Grouped spectrum for binned subject-level series

Source: R/grouped_spectrum.R
group_spectrum.Rd

Computes per-subject spectra and their group average for a regular matrix of subject-by-bin values or a grouped-series object.

Usage

group_spectrum(
  x,
  fs,
  flim = NULL,
  detrend = TRUE,
  detrend_order = 1L,
  pad_to = NULL,
  spectrum = c("amplitude", "raw_power"),
  taper = c("none", "hann", "hanning"),
  average = TRUE,
  fcor = FALSE
)

Arguments

x

numeric matrix with shape subjects x bins, or a list with fields data, subjects, and bins.

fs

sampling rate in Hz along the bin axis.

flim

optional length-2 frequency bounds.

detrend

logical; if TRUE, remove a linear trend from each subject series before spectral estimation.

detrend_order

non-negative integer polynomial order used when detrend = TRUE. The default 1 removes a linear trend, while higher orders remove higher-order polynomial structure.

pad_to

optional FFT length used for zero-padding each subject series before spectral estimation.

spectrum

spectrum scaling passed to spectral_peak.

taper

taper passed to spectral_peak.

average

logical; if TRUE, include the group-average spectrum.

fcor

logical; apply 1/f correction to each subject spectrum.

Value

list with observed and meta fields.
